display.purpose: Hero heading only. One or two short lines maximum. The font choice at this size is the loudest design statement on the site.

headline.purpose: Section and page-level headings. Maintains the mono voice at a step below display.

title.purpose: Project names and sub-section titles. The transition from mono to Figtree signals a shift from announcement to content.

body.purpose: Hooks, descriptions, and all continuous prose. Maximum 65-75ch line length.

label.purpose: Section metadata, attribute names (@frameworks, @live), framework badges, prompt prefixes. The only role where tracking (+0.05em) is used.

code.purpose: Inline code references and code block content. Shiki handles syntax coloring.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default easing for state transitions (hover, focus).

motion.name: ease-decelerate
motion.purpose: Elements entering the screen. Decelerates into rest position.

motion.name: duration-fast
motion.purpose: Hover color changes, focus rings, micro-interactions.

motion.name: duration-standard
motion.purpose: Panel reveals, state changes with visible displacement.

motion.name: background-lerp
motion.purpose: The canvas dot grid follows the cursor with a 0.1 lerp factor — smooth but not elastic. This is the system's primary interactive texture.

components.name: Brand Mark
components.description: The \\ double-backslash structural mark. Always Signal Amber, always JetBrains Mono, always aria-hidden when decorative.
components.css: .ds-brand-mark { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: oklch(0.6823 0.176 46.72); letter-spacing: -0.02em; line-height: 1; flex-shrink: 0; }

components.name: Section Divider
components.description: The full structural pattern: Brand Mark | hr | Circuit Purple label | Brand Mark. Opens each major section.
components.html:
\\

applications

\\
components.css: .ds-section-divider { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 1rem; } .ds-section-divider__rule { flex: 1; border: none; border-top: 1px solid oklch(1 0 0 / 10%); margin: 0; } .ds-section-divider__label { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 400; color: oklch(0.5579 0.0909 318.07); letter-spacing: 0.05em; flex-shrink: 0; margin: 0; } .ds-brand-mark { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: oklch(0.6823 0.176 46.72); letter-spacing: -0.02em; line-height: 1; flex-shrink: 0; }

components.name: Button Link (Primary CTA)
components.description: Link-style CTA. No background container. Signal Amber at rest; underline on hover. The system's primary interactive affordance.
components.css: .ds-btn-link { color: oklch(0.6823 0.176 46.72); font-family: 'Figtree', sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 400; line-height: 1.75; text-decoration: underline; text-underline-offset: 4px; text-decoration-color: transparent; transition: text-decoration-color 150ms ease; padding: 0; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; display: inline; } .ds-btn-link:hover { text-decoration-color: oklch(0.6823 0.176 46.72); } .ds-btn-link:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(0.6823 0.176 46.72); outline-offset: 2px; border-radius: 2px; }

components.name: Install Command
components.description: Package install command display. Muted surface container, Circuit Purple prompt operator, Console Blue command text.
components.html: $dotnet add package Waystone.Monads
components.css: .ds-install-command { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.75rem; background-color: oklch(0.268 0.007 34.298); border-radius: 0.325rem; padding: 0.625rem 1rem; } .ds-install-command__prompt { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 400; color: oklch(0.5579 0.0909 318.07); user-select: none; line-height: 1.25rem; } .ds-install-command__text { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 400; color: oklch(0.8877 0.0288 218.97); line-height: 1.25rem; }

components.name: Framework Badge
components.description: Technology version chip. Muted surface, Border Subtle outline, Console Blue JetBrains Mono text.
components.css: .ds-framework-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; background-color: oklch(0.268 0.007 34.298); color: oklch(0.8877 0.0288 218.97); font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 400; padding: 0.25rem 0.5rem; border-radius: 0.325rem; border: 1px solid oklch(1 0 0 / 10%); line-height: 1; white-space: nowrap; }

components.name: Project Attribute Label
components.description: The @label metadata pattern. Circuit Purple JetBrains Mono label. Value follows in Console Blue Figtree.
components.css: .ds-project-attr-list { display: grid; grid-template-columns: auto 1fr; gap: 0.25rem 1rem; align-items: baseline; margin: 0; } .ds-project-attr { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 400; color: oklch(0.5579 0.0909 318.07); letter-spacing: 0.025em; margin: 0; } .ds-project-attr-value { font-family: 'Figtree', sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 400; color: oklch(0.8877 0.0288 218.97); margin: 0; }

components.name: Code Block Container
components.description: Shiki-powered code block shell. Muted Surface background, rounded-lg. Shiki handles syntax via dual-theme CSS custom properties.
components.css: .ds-code-block { background-color: oklch(0.268 0.007 34.298); border-radius: 0.45rem; padding: 1.25rem 1.5rem; overflow-x: auto; } .ds-code-block__code { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 400; line-height: 1.6; color: oklch(0.8877 0.0288 218.97); white-space: pre; margin: 0; }

components.name: Hero Heading
components.description: The site's primary display type. JetBrains Mono at display scale. The second span uses Circuit Purple for the employer qualifier.
components.css: .ds-hero-heading { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: clamp(2.5rem, 6vw, 4.5rem); font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: -0.02em; line-height: 1.1; margin: 0; } .ds-hero-heading__primary { color: oklch(0.8877 0.0288 218.97); } .ds-hero-heading__qualifier { color: oklch(0.5579 0.0909 318.07); }

narrative.overview:
This system is built around the idea of a deliverable: structured, authoritative, and designed to reward both a 30-second skim and a careful read. It is not a showcase — it is a document that happens to run in a browser. Every element earns its placement the same way a well-structured architecture decision record earns its bullet points: by carrying information, not decoration.

The aesthetic is dark by necessity, not by trend. The audience (engineers, technical recruiters, hiring managers) arrives from a focused working context. The site meets them where they are: dark surface, high-contrast reading text, monospace headings that signal technical precision before a word is read. Density is calibrated above sparse but below overwhelming — the goal is substance, not noise.

What this system explicitly rejects: blocky card grids that flatten everything to the same visual weight, résumé-dump chronology without editorial selection, scroll-hijacking or motion that exists to demonstrate capability rather than convey information, glassmorphism, and the leerob.com-style bareness that reads as stripped rather than curated.
keyCharacteristics: Monospace display type announces technical authority; humanist body type explains it
Three-voice color system: amber draws the eye, purple structures the page, blue carries the content
The \\ double backslash is structural punctuation — it marks where sections begin and end
Interactivity is purposeful: hover states convey state, nothing animates without meaning
Code blocks are first-class visual elements, not afterthoughts

rules.name: The Three-Voice Rule
rules.body: Every foreground element speaks in one of three voices: Signal Amber (look here), Circuit Purple (this is structure), Console Blue (this is content). A fourth foreground color requires explicit justification.

rules.name: The Amber Scarcity Rule
rules.body: Signal Amber appears on no more than 15% of any given screen. Its rarity is what makes it work as an eye-catcher. When amber is everywhere, the signal is noise.

rules.name: The No-Purple-Brandmark Rule
rules.body: The \\ mark always renders in Signal Amber. Never Circuit Purple. Purple is for labels; amber is the mark.

rules.name: The Inversion Rule
rules.body: Display and all labels are mono; prose is humanist. A Figtree headline is the wrong signal. A JetBrains Mono body paragraph is unreadable at length and defeats the point of the pairing.

rules.name: The One-Pair Rule
rules.body: This system uses exactly two typefaces. No display serif, no third typeface for special callouts.

rules.name: The No-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Box shadows are prohibited throughout the system. Depth comes from tonal surface steps and spacing. A shadow here would read as a design mistake, not a feature.

dos: Use Signal Amber exclusively for the \\ brand mark, primary CTAs, and primary display headings.
Set all display headings and section labels in JetBrains Mono — even when the label feels too technical for the context.
Write project hooks in first person: 'I built this because…', 'I wanted to…'.
Treat code blocks as first-class visual content — they do as much persuasive work as the prose beside them.
Respect prefers-reduced-motion on every custom animation.
Prefix section labels with the \\ brand mark on both sides of the divider rule.
Use the @label attribute convention for project metadata: @frameworks, @live, @repository, @targets. Lowercase, mono, Circuit Purple.
donts: Don't use glassmorphism, blurred card surfaces, or frosted overlays.
Don't introduce blocky card grids with identical cards at the same visual weight.
Don't add scroll-hijacking, parallax layers, or entrance animations that gate content visibility.
Don't use Circuit Purple for the \\ brand mark.
Don't treat the site as a résumé dump — fewer projects with more depth.
Don't use filled button containers in the primary content flow.
Don't add box shadows. The No-Shadow Rule is system-wide and absolute.
Don't use leerob.com-style bareness — content density is part of this site's voice.
